case_name: Jalena Taylor and Brian Taylor v. Premier Women's Health, PLLC and Donielle Daigle, M. D.

summary.brief: Jalena Taylor and Brian Taylor v. Premier Women's Health, PLLC and Donielle Daigle, M. D.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: On February 17, 2017, Jalena was admitted to Memorial Hospital of Gulfport in active labor preparing to give birth.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After pushing for two and a half hours, the baby’s head became lodged in the mother’s pelvis, and it was determined that a caesarean section was necessary.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Following delivery of the child, Jalena’s blood pressure dropped, and her pulse increased.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Jalena was given a drug to tighten the uterus, and an OR team was called to be on standby in the event surgery became necessary.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Daigle called the OR team off after Jalena’s bleeding was minimal, and her uterus remained completely firm.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Daigle initiated an incision for exploratory laparotomy, and the uterus was exteriorized.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The operative report notes: her uterus was still very atonic.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Daigle and Dr. Ware made one last attempt directed at the uterine atony before proceeding with a hysterectomy by placing a liquid-filled balloon vaginally.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The balloon was unsuccessful, and the abdominal approach was resumed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury returned a twelve-to-zero verdict in favor of Dr. Daigle and Premier.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Their expert, Dr. Jeffrey Koren, testified that the standard of care was not breached.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: We affirm the verdict of the jury because it was reached on factual evidence in favor of Dr. Daigle and Premier by an impartial jury.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Jalena was admitted to Memorial Hospital of Gulfport in active labor. After pushing for two and a half hours, the baby’s head became lodged in the mother’s pelvis, necessitating a caesarean section.

requestables.name: OB/GYN consult
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Daigle regarding postpartum hemorrhage

reveal.result_summary: Dr. Daigle assessed the situation and initially managed with medication for uterine atony.
reveal.clinical_significance: Initial management focused on uterine atony.
reveal.decision_impact: Led to decision to monitor rather than immediate surgical intervention.

requestables.name: Exploratory laparotomy
requestables.description: Surgical exploration to address ongoing bleeding

reveal.result_summary: Uterus was exteriorized; atony persisted despite medication.

timeline_phases.name: Labor and Delivery
timeline_phases.description: Patient admitted in active labor, leading to caesarean section.

timeline_phases.name: Postpartum Hemorrhage Management
timeline_phases.description: Management of postpartum hemorrhage with medication and monitoring.

key_events.event: Administration of medication for uterine atony

timeline_phases.name: Surgical Intervention
timeline_phases.description: Exploratory laparotomy and subsequent procedures to control bleeding.

decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing postpartum hemorrhage?

options.description: Perform a thorough vaginal examination to rule out lacerations
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care requires ruling out genital tract lacerations in cases of postpartum hemorrhage.

options.description: Administer medication for uterine atony and monitor
options.clinical_reasoning: Initial management focused on uterine atony without thorough examination for lacerations.

actual_action_defendant.description: Administered medication for uterine atony and monitored
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed uterine atony was the sole cause of bleeding.

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated this was necessary to rule out lacerations.

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to perform a thorough examination could miss a laceration causing bleeding.
explanation.what_should_have_happened: A thorough examination should have been performed to rule out lacerations.
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony.

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent hysterectomy due to uncontrolled bleeding.

key_findings: Jury found no breach of standard of care.
Bleeding was attributed to uterine atony, not a missed laceration.

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Alleged failure to perform thorough examination
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Not applicable as no breach was found.

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to hospital in active labor

diagnoses.diagnosis: Postpartum hemorrhage due to uterine atony

treatments.treatment: Administration of medication for uterine atony

allegations.allegation: Failure to perform thorough vaginal examination

court_findings.finding: No breach of standard of care
court_findings.legal_significance: Jury verdict in favor of Dr. Daigle
